decision_point: In an adult undergoing major reconstructive orthopedic or trauma surgery, is prophylactic intravenous Tranexamic Acid or standard care (no TXA) preferred to reduce blood loss?
clinical_vignette:
Typical adult patient for whom this decision arises (no atypical comorbidities or contraindications).

Decision: In an adult undergoing major reconstructive orthopedic or trauma surgery, is prophylactic intravenous Tranexamic Acid or standard care (no TXA) preferred to reduce blood loss?
Options:
  1. Standard care (no TXA)
  2. Prophylactic Tranexamic Acid
label: settled
label_rationale: Evidenced by CRASH-2 and clinical guidelines, prophylactic intravenous TXA significantly reduces blood loss and transfusion requirements without elevating thromboembolic risks.
